crossRefs.label: Purge control SOL.V. in installed condition

blocks.text: Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat.
Disconnect the drain air tube (A) from the canister and attach the hose (B) and hand vacuum pump (C).
Remove the fuel filler cap and relieve the pressure in the fuel tank.
Start the engine, operate the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.

Leave the car idling and confirm with HDS that the purge control SOL.V. in the data list is open. After the purge control SOL.V. valve is opened, check that the negative pressure gauge on the hand vacuum pump gradually changes to the intake manifold negative pressure.

items.text: If the negative pressure changes, proceed to step "Negative Pressure Maintenance Check".
If the negative pressure changes, proceed to step "Negative Pressure Maintenance Check".
If the negative pressure does not change, remove the hand vacuum pump from the hose and proceed to step "Purge Line Inspection".
If the negative pressure does not change, remove the hand vacuum pump from the hose and proceed to step "Purge Line Inspection".

blocks.text: Put the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode and check that negative pressure is maintained.

refs.label: Purge control SOL.V. in installed condition

items.text: If negative pressure is maintained, fuel evaporative gas emission deterrent device normal.
If negative pressure is maintained, fuel evaporative gas emission deterrent device normal.
If negative pressure is not maintained, inspect the following items
If negative pressure is not maintained, inspect the following items
2-way valve leaking

refs.label: Purge control SOL.V. in installed condition

items.text: 2-way valve leaking
2-way valve leaking
Torn hose between canister and 2-way valve
Torn hose between canister and 2-way valve
Torn negative pressure tube between canister and purge control sol.v.
Torn negative pressure tube between canister and purge control sol.v.
Purge control SOL.V. in installed condition

refs.label: Purge control SOL.V. in installed condition

items.text: Purge control SOL.V. in installed condition

refs.label: Purge control SOL.V. in installed condition

blocks.text: Disconnect the negative pressure tube (A) from the canister, connect the hand vacuum pump (B), and verify that negative pressure is generated.

items.text: If there is no negative pressure, the purge control SOL.V. is in operation and Installed state Inspect the negative pressure tube between the canister and the purge control sol.v. for tears, blockages, and ports on the intake manifold.

If there is no negative pressure, the purge control SOL.V. is in operation and Installed state Inspect the negative pressure tube between the canister and the purge control sol.v. for tears, blockages, and ports on the intake manifold.
If there is negative pressure, Replace canister and re-inspect.
If there is negative pressure, Replace canister and re-inspect.
